content: Butler has named former Bulldogs player Ronald Nored to succeed Thad Matta as head coach, the school announced Wednesday.

title: LIV sued by distillery for trademark infringement
content: A Long Island-based distillery sued LIV Golf in federal court on Monday, alleging the golf league's use of "LIV" branding for alcoholic beverages and apparel infringes on its registered trademark.

title: L.A. spoils Tiger's return, cruises to TGL title
content: Tiger Woods competed for the first time in more than a year and that wasn't nearly enough to stop Los Angeles Golf Club, which made three straight eagles to close out a 9-2 victory in the TGL finals Tuesday night to capture the SoFi Cup.

title: Elite longevity: Flacco, 41, agrees to Cincy deal
content: Quarterback Joe Flacco is coming back for a 19th NFL season, agreeing to a one-year, $6 million deal with the Bengals, sources told ESPN's Jeremy Fowler.

title: Shiffrin wins record-tying 6th World Cup title
content: Skier Mikaela Shiffrin held off a challenge from Germany's Emma Aicher to win a record-tying sixth women's overall World Cup skiing title on Wednesday in Norway.

title: Sources: Crow-Armstrong deal for 6 years, $115M
content: Pete Crow-Armstrong's new contract extension with the Chicago Cubs is for six years and $115 million, sources told ESPN's Jeff Passan on Tuesday.

title: NBA players' union calls for 65-game rule change
content: The NBPA is calling on the league to, at minimum, amend the 65-game rule for players to be eligible for end-of-season awards.

content: Cristiano Ronaldo has five Ballon d'Ors and Thierry Henry will forever be an "Invincible," but neither had a Premier League career to match Mohamed Salah's.

content: Refugees are officially not allowed to work in Bangladesh, and smaller rations make survival even more difficult for Rohingyas.

content: The woman, identified as Linsula, was originally from Nagaland's Kiphire district and worked at a spa in Chikkamagaluru.

content: The programme aims to develop 100 airports by upgrading existing unserved airstrips, supported by a capital outlay of Rs 12,159 crore.

content: While Trump is deploying warships and troops to the Middle East, about 59 per cent of Americans say US military action in Iran has been excessive.

title: Kenya Flower Industry Loses $1.4 Million Weekly Due To US-Iran-Israel War
content: While the Middle East isn't Kenya's main export market for flowers, the US-Iran war has disrupted its cargo freight to Europe, leading to huge losses.

content: The presence of these structures suggests that the modern city of Puri sits directly atop a much older, sophisticated urban layout.

content: In a stern warning to the US forces, Iran's parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, said that sending troops to the region will not solve what the US and Israeli leadership have botched.

content: The CJI said someone called his brother and asked how he could pass "such an order" during a hearing on medical admission-related case.

content: The event draws the presence of several leaders from the NDA, who extend their blessings to the newly married couple

content: Two of the personnel have been placed under the custody of the central forces, while the remaining five have been transferred out of the State, he said

title: A.P. CM unveils roadmap to diversify tourism options; panel to fast-track projects
content: Naidu instructs officials to rapidly scale up infrastructure in line with the rising influx of tourists; target set to ready 50,000 hotel rooms and 10,000 homestays by 2029

title: Toll charges in Karnataka to rise from April 1
content: Data from the Ministry of Road Transport and Highways, accessed by The Hindu, shows that Karnataka has witnessed a sharp increase in user fee collections over the past five years. Collections have nearly doubled from ₹1,866 crore in 2020–21 to over ₹4,320 crore in 2024–25

title: Fuel crisis LIVE: Consumer protection watchdog warns restaurants against levying gas, LPG charge
content: CCPA made it clear that only applicable taxes can be added to the price mentioned in the menu of restaurants and hotels; all input costs should be factored into the prices displayed in the menu

title: ‘AI Fusion’ summit in Vijayawada on April 4
content: It provides a comprehensive overview of the Al landscape, and experts from leading global corporations will give valuable insights into how AI is shaping the world and where it is headed, say organisers

title: Police suspect foul play in Amaravati fire accidents
content: Preliminary investigation indicates that the fire originated directly in the plastic pipe storage area of Megha Engineering and Infrastructures Limited, suggesting deliberate human involvement, says Guntur SP

title: Over 10,000 handloom weavers in A.P. benefit from NHDP
content: The Ministry of Textiles assures second-phase aid for ongoing projects; The Andhra Pradesh State has 1,27,662 handloom weavers as per the Fourth All India Handloom Census-2020.

title: Assembly Elections LIVE: ECI hikes remuneration for poll officials ahead of Assembly elections in 5 States
content: The daily allowance for presiding officers has been increased from ₹350 earlier to ₹500 per day, or a total of ₹2,000 for the assignment, Election Commission official said

title: No power tariff hike for consumers in Andhra Pradesh for 2026-27 financial year
content: State government undertakes to bridge the estimated revenue gap of the Discoms amounting to nearly ₹15,791 crore, says A.P. Electricity Regulatory Commission

title: IIM-K breaks into global top 100 in QS rankings
content: The institute has been ranked 78th worldwide in the narrow subject category of Business and Management Studies, marking a rise from 141st position last year

title: CPI MP flags “grave breach” as BJP seal appears on official EC document
content: P. Sandosh Kumar seeks time‑bound probe into Kerala CEO’s actions, cites threat to EC’s neutrality and public trust

title: Untreated waste, poor civic infra mar Erode (East) Assembly constituency
content: Pollution, traffic congestion, and poor urban infrastructure remain key concerns for voters, who are looking for concrete outcomes

title: On Consumer Rights Day, Consumer panel urges public to check ISI marks on goods
content: ‘The presence of such quality certification marks indicates that the products meet quality standards and have been certified by the government before entering the market’

title: Venues serving liquor must initiate rigorous age verification protocols, says Karnataka High Court
content: When minors gain entry and order intoxicants, whether overtly served or covertly consumed, the management of the places would be held accountable for any lapses, the court noted

title: T.N. election 2026: Kurinjipadi constituency voters want non-polluting, agro-based industrial boost
content: Minister MRK Panneerselvam is in the fray aiming for a hat-trick in Kurinjipadi. The DMK-led Democratic Progressive Alliance needs to be aware that the Tamizhaga Vazhvurimai Katchi headed by T. Velmurugan and now delinked from the DMK-led alliance, is capable of eating into the votes of the DMK in areas like Kurinjipadi and Kullanchavadi where it has significant presence

title: Legislative Assembly likely to meet on March 28, pass resolution on Amaravati as ‘sole and permanent capital’ of Andhra Pradesh
content: The special session follows the Union government’s move to grant statutory status to Amaravati, aiming to end years of legal and political uncertainty over the issue

title: Parliament Budget Session Highlights: Parliament passes Transgender Rights Amendment Bill, 2026
content: The Rajya Sabha negates referring the Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, to a select committee of the Rajya Sabha; Both Houses adjourn for the day

title: Budget: Property tax push drives MCC’s ₹1,311 crore revenue plan
content: Regional Commissioner and MCC administrator Nitesh Patil presents Mysuru City Corporation’s 2026–27 budget; ₹10.20 crore surplus projected

title: Execution of RE projects brooks no delay, Energy Minister tells officials
content: Ensure the projects are commissioned as per committed schedules and proactively remove bottlenecks, he says, and advises investors to bring to the government’s notice any ground-level challenges for prompt resolution

title: Bills' massive bison statues anchor Highmark Stadium's Family Circle in new renderings
content: the bison statues and the Family Circle are a part of an area that will be a first welcome to the new stadium . the installation will feature three stainless steel bison -- a bull, cow and calf -- modeled after the American bison .

title: What's inside Trump's 15-point plan to end war with Iran?
content: The United States has proposed a 15-point plan to end the war with Iran, even as Tehran has denied any formal talks. The conflict, which began on February 28, has caused heavy casualties and disrupted global markets. The plan outlines a ceasefire and key conditions, but there is no official confirmation yet. Iran has rejected US claims of negotiations and set its own conditions, including recognition of its rights, reparations and security guarantees, while also seeking sanctions relief and other measures.

title: All-party meet on Middle East: Opposition backs govt steps in crisis, says Rijiju
content: Opposition parties pledged full support to the government on the Middle East crisis following an all-party meeting. Ministers addressed concerns regarding the Iran-Israel-US conflict's impact on India, including energy supplies and citizen safety. While most parties expressed satisfaction, some, like the TMC, skipped the meeting, and a few voiced lingering concerns about parliamentary debate.

title: Assam poll SWOT analysis: Hold or flip for Himanta vs Gogoi?
content: Assam's political landscape is heating up alongside spring festivities, with the BJP aiming for a solo majority against a resurgent Congress. Key contests include Himanta Biswa Sarma versus Gaurav Gogoi, while issues like identity, infiltration, and development shape the electoral battleground. Post-delimitation changes add another layer to this defining election.

title: Meta eyes biggest-ever layoffs: What HR’s email told employees
content: Meta is preparing for its largest-ever round of layoffs, with employees in wearables and ads told to work from home as cuts loom. Reuters reported the reductions could affect 20% of its nearly 79,000-strong workforce—around 15,800 jobs. The move is driven by Meta's $600 billion AI infrastructure commitment and a broader push toward leaner, AI-assisted operations, compounded by setbacks with its in-house AI models.

title: Sonia Gandhi in hospital: Doctors say veteran Congress leader to remain under observation
content: Veteran Congress leader Sonia Gandhi is stable and under observation at Sir Ganga Ram Hospital after being admitted Tuesday night due to feeling unwell following a weather change. Doctors are conducting investigations for potential stomach and urinary tract infections, with antibiotics administered as part of her treatment.  Her condition is being closely monitored by a medical team.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ box office day 7 live: Ranveer Singh starrer chases Rs 700 cr
content: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' featuring Ranveer Singh and directed by Aditya Dhar has cemented its status as an all-time blockbuster, crossing the monumental Rs. 900 crore gross milestone worldwide in its first week. Despite a massive 3-hour and 49-minute runtime, the espionage thriller has maintained an iron grip on the domestic market, fueled by strong word-of-mouth and festive boosts from Ugadi and Gudi Padwa. From its record-breaking Rs. 114.85 crore peak on Day 4 to its current global gross of Rs. 919 crore, 'Dhurandhar 2' continues to shatter records as the fastest Indian film to breach the Rs. 500 crore mark.

title: Kevin O'Leary slams US government: You should have allowed Intel to die
content: Canadian billionaire Kevin O'Leary slammed US government funding for Intel after a robot malfunctioned, blaming subsidized chips. He suggested robots need safety features like a 'kill switch.' This criticism emerges as humanoid robots, from Tesla to Figure AI and Xiaomi, advance rapidly, sparking debates on reliability and government tech investment.

title: England cricket explodes: ECB hits back at batter's 'no one cares' claim
content: Livingstone, who has not played international cricket since England’s winless campaign in the ICC Champions Trophy under former captain Jos Buttler, had told ESPNCricinfo that the management, including Key and head coach Brendon McCullum, did not show enough concern for players once they were dropped.

title: War-hit schedule sees Iran's Tractor travel to Saudi for ACL quarterfinal berth
content: Iran’s Tractor FC will travel to Saudi Arabia to face Shabab Al Ahli in a playoff. The winner will move into the quarterfinals of the Asian Champions League Elite, where they will meet Buriram United.

title: Majority view US strikes on Iran as excessive; gas price fears rise: Poll
content: A new poll reveals a majority of Americans believe recent US military actions against Iran have gone too far, despite growing concerns over rising fuel costs. While preventing Iran from acquiring nuclear weapons is a key objective, most oppose further escalation, including ground forces, as the US deploys additional troops to the Middle East.

title: Tesla ex-VP shares AI coding horror that could have wiped sensitive data; Musk agrees
content: A compromised version of the popular AI library LiteLLM, with 97 million monthly downloads, briefly turned pip installs into a credential theft operation. The malicious package, live for two hours, was only detected due to a bug causing a system crash. This incident highlights the risks of extensive dependency chains in software development.

title: Samuthirakani responds to viral Vijay-Trisha remarks, says it was concern, not criticism
content: Actor Samuthirakani addresses a viral video controversy. He explains his remarks about Vijay and Trisha were taken out of context. Samuthirakani insists his words came from genuine concern and emotion. He maintains he speaks honestly and has no regrets about his statements. The actor clarifies his perspective on public appearances.

title: Facing racism in Indian dressing room, how Sivaramakrishnan found comfort in WI
content: Sivaramakrishnan, who played 25 international matches for India, made his Test debut in Antigua in April 1983 at the age of 17 years and 118 days, becoming one of the youngest to represent the country in the format.

title: ‘No shortage of fuel’: Centre assures LPG, diesel and petrol supply; pushes PNG, kerosene
content: Centre on Wednesday said there was no shortage of LPG, petrol or diesel in the country and urged people not to panic amid reports of increased demand in some areas. Officials said PNG supply was being prioritised and CNG was fully available for transport. The government also highlighted enforcement action against hoarding, with thousands of raids conducted and cylinders seized, while additional kerosene allocations have been made as an alternative fuel.

title: 'Talking bulls**t' about Arjun Tendulkar': Yuvraj Singh's father blasts R Ashwin
content: R Ashwin’s claim that Arjun Tendulkar won’t feature for Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2026 due to strong competition has sparked controversy. Yograj Singh hit back sharply, calling his remarks “bulls**t” and questioning his authority. He defended Arjun’s potential, highlighting past issues and boldly claiming he could transform him into a top batter within six months.

title: 'Finally': Dutch No. 1 Anish Giri jabs FIDE over unusual Cyprus safety post
content: Concerns over safety and infrastructure in Cyprus ahead of the Candidates have unsettled players, with Koneru Humpy withdrawing and Hikaru Nakamura questioning power reliability. Anish Giri mocked FIDE’s delayed response. FIDE has now issued a FAQ, insisting Cyprus is safe, outages are rare, and backup systems and contingency plans are in place to ensure smooth conduct.

title: Happy Ram Navami 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes to share with your family and friends
content: Ram Navami, celebrating Lord Ram's birth, is a significant Hindu festival observed on the ninth day of Chaitra month. In 2026, it falls on March 27th, marked by fasting, temple visits, and devotional practices. The festival symbolizes victory over evil and upholds truth and dharma, offering a time to connect with loved ones through heartfelt wishes and messages.

title: Bank holiday on Ram Navmi: Are banks open or closed on March 26 & 27
content: Bank customers must check holiday schedules before visiting branches this week. Closures will occur due to festivals and state holidays.  RBI guidelines also mean branches will be shut on the second and fourth Saturdays of March.  Plan your banking needs accordingly. Digital platforms like mobile apps and UPI remain active for everyday transactions.  Services requiring branch visits will be unavailable.

title: Indian universities clock record gains in QS subject rankings 2026
content: India has made significant strides in the QS World University Rankings by Subject 2026, with 99 institutions featuring 599 times across 55 disciplines. Forty-four percent of entries improved, the highest among large systems, while 120 are new. IIT Bombay and IIT Delhi entered the top 50 in Computer Science, and AIIMS reached its highest-ever Medicine ranking (105th). Business, engineering, and emerging disciplines show notable growth, while Arts & Humanities remain the weakest area, highlighting India’s expanding but uneven global higher education presence.

title: Barred by interim govt, BCB now wants Hasina-linked cricketer back for 'long term'
content: Shakib, 39, had moved to the United States after the country's Awami League government led by Sheikh Hasina was removed following a student uprising in 2024. The interim government had barred Shakib from representing Bangladesh

title: Pakistan train derailment: 25 injured as Tezgam Express coaches go off track
content: A train derailment in Pakistan's Punjab province has left at least 25 people injured. Seven coaches of the Tezgam Express went off the tracks near Lodhran on late Tuesday night. Rescue teams quickly responded to the scene. Authorities are investigating the cause, with human error and mechanical issues being considered

title: US deploys 2,000 paratroopers from ‘Immediate Response Force’ to Middle East: Report
content: Around 2,000 elite paratroopers are heading to the Middle East, bolstering US military options as President Trump explores diplomatic avenues with Iran. This deployment, alongside thousands of Marines, signals a significant escalation. The troops could be positioned for operations near Iran's oil hub, Kharg Island, or to secure vital shipping lanes.

title: 'Historic step': After Uttarakhand, Gujarat passes Uniform Civil Code Bill
content: Gujarat has passed its Uniform Civil Code Bill, 2026. This makes it the second Indian state to adopt a unified law for marriage, divorce, and inheritance. Union Home Minister Amit Shah hailed it as a historic step for equality. Chief Minister Bhupendra Patel stated the UCC will ensure equal rights and dignity for women across all communities.

title: Inside R Madhavan’s homes: Rs 17.5 cr Mumbai flat, Chennai bungalow, Dubai house
content: Actor R. Madhavan's impressive real estate portfolio, spanning Mumbai, Chennai, and Dubai, showcases a blend of luxury and purpose.  His homes, valued in crores, feature serene interiors, meditation spaces, and even hydroponic gardens, reflecting a life of comfort and sophisticated calm.  These properties underscore his strong financial standing, built on successful film projects and endorsements.

title: Born without arms or legs: What this 43-year-old man did to become inspiration for millions
content: Born without arms or legs in 1982, Nick Vujicic defied expectations to become a globally renowned motivational speaker. His message, forged through early struggles with bullying and isolation, emphasizes endurance, faith, and the persistent choice to keep going, transforming personal hardship into a powerful public mission.

title: 60 m barrels of Russian crude booked by Indian refiners amid global supply strain
content: Indian refiners have significantly increased purchases of Russian crude, securing around 60 million barrels for the upcoming month amidst Middle East conflict-driven supply disruptions. This surge is supported by a US waiver allowing imports of Russian oil loaded before March 12, with refiners also diversifying with Venezuelan and previously Iranian crude.

title: When is Ram Navami: March 26 or 27? Check correct date
content: Ram Navami, celebrating the birth of Lord Shri Ram, the seventh incarnation of Lord Vishnu, will be observed on March 26, 2026. This prominent Hindu festival marks the end of Chaitra Navratri and is celebrated with prayers, recitations of Ramacharitmanas, and acts of charity, including Kanya Pujan.

title: 'No shortage of petrol, diesel, or LPG': Oil firms soothe fuel concerns in India
content: Major oil companies have debunked claims of a fuel shortage, assuring the public that supplies are normal and sufficient. Despite global supply tensions due to the Middle East crisis, firms like BPCL, HPCL, and Indian Oil emphasize no panic buying is needed. They are committed to uninterrupted service, with real-time updates confirming smooth operations at retail outlets.

title: Man goes to spit tobacco near railway tracks, dies after being hit by Vande Bharat
content: A man lost his life after being struck by the Patna-Howrah Vande Bharat Express at Barh railway station. The incident occurred on platform number 2. He was taken to Barh Sadar Hospital but succumbed to his injuries. The deceased's identity remains unestablished. Railway authorities have urged passengers to maintain a safe distance from platform edges.

title: Govt mandates switch to piped gas: Centre orders transition to PNG wherever available
content: Households in areas with available piped natural gas (PNG) infrastructure must switch from LPG within three months or face supply cessation. This new policy, aimed at enhancing energy security and diversifying fuel sources amidst global disruptions, streamlines pipeline approvals and encourages a transition to PNG for continuous supply. Exceptions exist for technically infeasible connections.

title: 'Adequate stock available': Govt issues clarification on LPG refill booking timeline
content: Government has debunked false reports circulating on social media about revised LPG refill booking timelines.  Existing booking periods of 25 days in urban and 45 days in rural areas remain unchanged.  Authorities assure sufficient fuel stocks amidst Middle East tensions and urge the public to avoid panic buying and misinformation.

title: Dhoni hits Mukesh Chaudhary for a six; internet trolls with 'Isko koi bhi maar de' - watch
content: Veteran MS Dhoni, at 44, gears up for his 19th IPL season, showcasing his enduring finishing prowess in a recent net session.  Despite trolling for the bowler, Dhoni's continued impact is evident.  CSK, under Ruturaj Gaikwad, faces challenges with Nathan Ellis's injury but has bolstered their squad with Sanju Samson, signaling a future-focused approach.

title: Why US supercarrier had to leave Middle East amid Iran war: Many problems of USS Gerald Ford
content: The US Navy's most expensive aircraft carrier, USS Gerald R Ford, is facing renewed scrutiny after a fire forced its departure from a Middle East deployment. This incident highlights ongoing concerns about the $13.2 billion warship's operational readiness, with Pentagon testing revealing insufficient data on its combat effectiveness and reliability of key systems.

title: Cooper Connolly looks to learn, grow, and emulate Shaun Marsh at Punjab Kings
content: Perth-born Cooper Connolly, who has modelled his batting on Shaun Marsh, a former star for Punjab Kings (then Kings XI Punjab), is set to turn up for the Mohali-based franchise as he looks to emulate his idol. The 22-year-old was bought by Punjab Kings for Rs 3 crore during last year’s IPL mini auction.

title: Who is Harshil Kalia? Actress who died in a tragic car crash
content: Harshil Kalia, 30, Rajasthan actress-model, died in Jaipur crash on March 23. Car flipped on Shipra Path after losing control near 11:30 PM; locals rushed her to hospital, but head injuries fatal. Police probe speeding or overtake. CCTV out; body post family. Starred in Crime Next Door, music videos.

title: How to bet the Houston Open: Koepka stands out, but what about Gotterup?

title: Ranking all 52 NCAA tournament losses

title: Week 7 Top 25 and how to watch
content: the longhorns have won their past 26 games -- their only loss of the season came in early february . the other biggest mover in the top 10 was Florida, which won two of its three games against then-No. 1 Tennessee last week . there are no more undefeateds in the poll, with Grand Canyon losing its first game last Tuesday .

title: 'A lot of downside, but not a lot of upside': The balancing act facing Jayson Tatum and the Celtics

title: Ranking each team's top prospect: Hagens, Iginla, Martone, more

title: Feeling lucky? What turnovers and late-game success say about 2026
content: head coach golesh is pleased with the Tigers' energy and buy-in as they begin to take form as a team . he says coaches talk constantly about controlling what they can control . they know that even if they recruit well, develop players well and deploy sturdy tactics, sometimes the breaks are simply going to go against them .
